title: AHOL Benchmark Task
description: Schema for a single benchmark task instance loaded by packages/ahol/runner/benchmarks.py. AHOL's internal Task dataclass uses short field names (id, issue_body); this schema uses SWE-bench-style industry-standard names (instance_id, problem_statement) so payloads are self-documenting when emitted to logs, OpenTelemetry spans, or downstream verifier harnesses. validate_task() maps Task dataclass attributes to schema field names before validation.

instance_id.description: Unique benchmark task identifier, for example 'django__django-11099', 'swe-bench-live-2026-04-abc123', or 'BigCodeBench/42'. Used as the task_id foreign key in the task-runner-return contract.

problem_statement.description: The issue body, problem description, or natural-language spec that the variant agent must solve. Substituted into the Q1b patch-only system prompt at the {{ISSUE_BODY}} placeholder by packages/ahol/baseline/invoke.sh.

repo.description: Canonical repository identifier such as 'django/django', 'princeton-nlp/SWE-bench_Verified', or 'bigcode/bigcodebench-hard'. For algorithmic benchmarks without a real upstream repo (BigCodeBench-Hard), synthesize a stable dataset-qualified identifier.

base_commit.description: 40-character lowercase hex SHA-1 of the upstream commit the task is pinned to. For benchmarks without a real commit pin (BigCodeBench-Hard), synthesize via sha1(task_id) so every Task has a deterministic hex id suitable for later reproducibility checks.

benchmark_origin.description: Canonical HuggingFace dataset name this task was loaded from. Used by ahol.runner.ahol.run_task to route swebench --dataset_name when scoring the model patch. Examples: 'princeton-nlp/SWE-bench_Lite', 'princeton-nlp/SWE-bench_Verified', 'SWE-bench-Live/SWE-bench-Live', 'bigcode/bigcodebench-hard'. Loaders set it; the orchestrator never modifies it.

difficulty_bucket.description: Optional benchmark-specific difficulty label such as 'basic', 'advanced', 'expert', 'hard', or a numeric tier. Used for stratified sampling and variance analysis across difficulty cohorts.

expected_patch_size_range.description: Optional [min_bytes, max_bytes] hint for the expected diff size the variant agent should produce. Used for premature-termination detection in the Q1B validation checklist Gate 3 and Gate 4.

test_command.description: Optional verifier command invoked inside the benchmark container after the patch is applied, for example 'pytest tests/ -x' or 'go test ./...'. When absent, the benchmark harness uses the benchmark-native verifier.
